description: Creates a data table that evaluates mathematical formulas over a range of input values, displaying computed results in a labeled grid.
utility: Create a data table evaluating formulas over a range of values

arguments.name: position
arguments.description: Position on canvas using logical row, col coordinates

arguments.name: formulas
arguments.description: Column formulas as quoted strings - e.g., "x", "x^2", "sin(x)". Can also pass function references defined via funcdef. Supports function calls like "f(x+1)". Use comma to separate formula from display label: "f(x), f(x)=3x+1" evaluates f(x) but shows f(x)=3x+1 as header.

arguments.name: range
arguments.type: range(start, end) | range(start, end, step) | range(v1, v2, ...)
arguments.description: Values to evaluate: range(0, 5) for 0 to 5, range(0, 10, 2) for step of 2, or range(0.5, 0.9, 0.99) for explicit values

arguments.name: header
arguments.description: Custom column headers as quoted strings. If omitted, formula strings are used as headers.

arguments.name: color
arguments.description: Table color - e.g., c(blue), c(red), c(green)

arguments.name: fontSize
arguments.description: Font size for table cells - e.g., fs(16)

alternates.description: Table with x values from 0 to 5, computing x^2
Three-column table: x, x^2, and 2*x with step size 1
Table with hand-picked x-values, useful for limit exploration

alternates.syntax:
f(x) = x^2
T = table(at(row, col), "x", f, range(0, 5))
alternates.description: Column uses a previously defined function
Custom LaTeX headers separate from formula definitions
Comma separates the formula (before comma) from the display header (after comma). The formula is evaluated, the display label is shown in the header.
Table columns can use function calls with expressions like f(x+1). User-defined functions are available in the evaluation scope.

alternates.syntax:
f(x) = piecewise("1", "x >= -0.5 and x <= 0.5", "0")
T = table(at(row, col), "x", "f(x)", range(-2, 2, 0.5))
alternates.description: Table columns can reference piecewise functions defined via funcdef

examples.scenario: Table of x, log(x), e^x values
Table with function references for limit
Table comparing f(x) and f(x+1) with display labels
Table with piecewise function and function product
